task_completion.description: Verify the Oswego dataset is loaded in the analysis software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The statistical software (e.g., Epi Info) shows the 'Oswego' dataset loaded, typically indicated by a record count of 75 or the table name 'Oswego' visible in the interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the logistic regression is configured with the correct outcome and predictor variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A logistic regression dialog or command window shows 'ILL' selected as the Outcome variable, and at least 5 food items (e.g., VANILLA, CHOCOLATE, CABBAGE, BROWNBREAD, FRUITSALAD) included as terms/predictors.

task_completion.description: Verify the logistic regression analysis was executed and displays results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An output screen or window showing the results of the logistic regression, displaying Odds Ratios and P-Values for the included food items.

task_completion.description: Verify the analysis output is saved as an HTML file in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window showing 'logistic_output.html' located in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', or a browser/editor showing the saved HTML file with the regression results.

task_completion.description: Verify the text report identifies the correct significant risk factor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'logistic_report.txt' open from 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' that explicitly names 'VANILLA' (or Vanilla Ice Cream) as the significant risk factor or vehicle of infection.

task_completion.description: Verify the text report includes the Odds Ratio and a brief conclusion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The same 'logistic_report.txt' file contains the Odds Ratio for Vanilla (approximately 23.45) and a brief concluding sentence about it causing the outbreak.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used actual statistical software (like Epi Info) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GUI of a statistical analysis tool being interacted with, rather than just a Python script or terminal output mocking the results.

integrity.description: Ensure the HTML output was genuinely generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'logistic_output.html' should have the formatting and structure typical of an export from the statistical software used, not a manually typed basic HTML table.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just type the known answers into the text file without running the regression.
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be evidence of the regression actually being run (the output screen with the ~23.45 odds ratio) prior to the creation of the text report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct outcome variable is 'ILL'. The key risk factor causing the outbreak is 'VANILLA' (Vanilla Ice Cream). The true Odds Ratio for Vanilla is approximately 23.45, and it is statistically significant (P-Value < 0.05). The dataset contains 75 records.
